message:
feat(agent-sweep): implement parallel agent sweep with work-stealing (bd-2axv)

Replace stub with full parallel implementation:
- Work-stealing pattern with atomic queue dequeue via dir_lock
- Spawns up to max_parallel workers (default 4)
- Each worker processes repos from shared queue until empty
- Global rate limit backoff check before claiming work
- Falls back to sequential for single repo or --parallel=1
- Proper cleanup of work queue and lock directory

Also close bd-b00c (run_single_agent_workflow - already implemented)
and bd-ix9c (run_sequential_agent_sweep - already implemented).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,7 +85,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-209","title":"Implement dispatch_command()","description":"**Implement subcommand routing**

## What
Create the command dispatcher that routes to appropriate subcommand functions.

## Why
Subcommand architecture provides clean separation. The dispatcher is the central router.

## Implementation
```bash
dispatch_command() {
    local cmd="${1:-sync}"  # Default to sync
    shift || true

    case "$cmd" in
        sync)       cmd_sync "$@" ;;
        status)     cmd_status "$@" ;;
        init)       cmd_init "$@" ;;
        add)        cmd_add "$@" ;;
        list)       cmd_list "$@" ;;
        doctor)     cmd_doctor "$@" ;;
        self-update) cmd_self_update "$@" ;;
        config)     cmd_config "$@" ;;
        -h|--help)  show_help ;;
        -v|--version) show_version ;;
        *)
            # Maybe it's a file/URL passed directly
            if [[ -f "$cmd" || "$cmd" =~ ^https?:// ]]; then
                cmd_sync "$cmd" "$@"
            else
                log_error "Unknown command: $cmd"
                exit 4
            fi
            ;;
    esac
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands route correctly
- Default command is sync
- Unknown commands error with exit code 4
- Files/URLs passed directly trigger sync","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.327002117-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.327002117-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in initial ru script skeleton","labels":["cli","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-209","depends_on_id":"bd-208","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.212452531-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-210","title":"Implement parse_global_args() and main()","description":"**Implement argument parsing and main entry point**

## What
Parse global arguments (--json, --quiet, etc.) and create the main() entry point.

## Why
Global options apply to all commands and must be parsed before dispatch.

## Global Options
- --help, -h: Show help
- --version, -v: Show version
- --json: JSON output mode
- --quiet, -q: Minimal output
- --verbose: Detailed output
- --non-interactive: Never prompt
- --dry-run: Show what would happen

## Implementation Pattern
```bash
main() {
    # Parse global args first
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            --json) JSON_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --quiet|-q) QUIET_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --verbose) VERBOSE_MODE=true; shift ;;
            --non-interactive) INTERACTIVE=false; shift ;;
            --dry-run) DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
            --) shift; break ;;
            -*) break ;;  # Let dispatch handle unknown flags
            *) break ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Check gum availability
    check_gum
    
    # Dispatch to subcommand
    dispatch_command "$@"
}

main "$@"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Global args parsed before dispatch
- Flags correctly set runtime state
- Unknown args passed to subcommands
- Script runs when executed directly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.328450456-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:26.328450456-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in initial ru script skeleton","labels":["cli","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-210","depends_on_id":"bd-209","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.241926646-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2axv","title":"Implement run_parallel_agent_sweep()","description":"# Parallel Agent Sweep Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-eta6 (Parallel Processing & Work Queue)

## Purpose
Process multiple repositories concurrently using work-stealing pattern.

## Implementation

```bash
run_parallel_agent_sweep() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local parallel="$2"
    local with_release="$3"
    
    # Create work queue
    local work_queue=$(mktemp)
    local results_file="${RESULTS_FILE}"
    local lock_base="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks"
    mkdir -p "$lock_base"
    
    printf "%s\n" "${repos_ref[@]}" > "$work_queue"
    
    # Spawn workers
    local pids=()
    for ((i=0; i<parallel; i++)); do
        (
            while true; do
                local repo_spec=""
                
                # Check global backoff first
                agent_sweep_backoff_wait_if_needed
                
                # Atomic dequeue
                if dir_lock_acquire "${lock_base}/queue.lock" 30; then
                    if [[ -s "$work_queue" ]]; then
                        repo_spec=$(head -1 "$work_queue")
                        tail -n +2 "$work_queue" > "${work_queue}.tmp"
                        mv "${work_queue}.tmp" "$work_queue"
                    fi
                    dir_lock_release "${lock_base}/queue.lock"
                fi
                
                [[ -z "$repo_spec" ]] && break
                
                # Process repo
                local repo_name repo_path session_name
                repo_name=$(basename "$repo_spec" | sed "s/@.*//")
                repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
                session_name="ru_sweep_${repo_name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}_${$}_${i}"
                
                run_single_agent_workflow "$session_name" "$repo_path" "$with_release"
            done
        ) &
        pids+=($\!)
    done
    
    # Wait for all workers
    local exit_code=0
    for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
        wait "$pid" || exit_code=1
    done
    
    rm -f "$work_queue"
    return $exit_code
}
```

## Lock Points

| Lock | Purpose | Timeout |
|------|---------|---------|
| queue.lock | Atomic dequeue | 30s |
| results.lock | Atomic result append | 30s |
| backoff.lock | Global rate limit | 10s |

## Session Naming
ru_sweep_{repo_name}_{pid}_{worker_index}
Prevents collisions between parallel workers.

## ntm Serialization
Each repo has its own session, so no cross-repo serialization needed.

-{"id":"bd-b00c","title":"Implement run_single_agent_workflow()","description":"# Single Agent Workflow Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Execute the full three-phase agent workflow for a single repository.

## Implementation

```bash
run_single_agent_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local with_release="${2:-false}"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)

    # Preflight check
    if ! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "preflight" "skipped" 0 "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # ADDED: Create backup reference before any changes
    git -C "$repo_path" update-ref -m "agent-sweep backup before run $RUN_ID" \
        "refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-$RUN_ID" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true

    # Generate unique session name
    local session_name="ru_sweep_$(sanitize_session_name "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")")_$$"

    # Spawn session
    local spawn_result
    spawn_result=$(ntm_spawn_session "$session_name" "$repo_path")
    if ! json_get_field "$spawn_result" "success" | grep -q "true"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "spawn" "failed" 0 "session spawn failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Phase 1: Understanding
    local phase1_prompt
    phase1_prompt=$(get_phase1_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase1_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE1_TIMEOUT:-180}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase1" "timeout" "$PHASE1_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate understanding
    local pane_output
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local understanding
    understanding=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "UNDERSTANDING")

    # Phase 2: Commit Planning
    local phase2_prompt
    phase2_prompt=$(get_phase2_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase2_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE2_TIMEOUT:-300}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase2" "timeout" "$PHASE2_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate commit plan
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local commit_plan
    commit_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "COMMIT_PLAN")

    if ! validate_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "validation" "failed" 0 "commit plan validation failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Execute commit plan (if apply mode)
    if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
        if ! execute_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "execution" "failed" 0 "commit execution failed" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Phase 3: Release (optional)
    if [[ "$with_release" == true ]] && has_release_workflow "$repo_path"; then
        local phase3_prompt
        phase3_prompt=$(get_phase3_prompt "$repo_path")
        ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase3_prompt"

        if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE3_TIMEOUT:-600}"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase3" "timeout" "$PHASE3_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi

        # Extract and execute release plan
        pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
        local release_plan
        release_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "RELEASE_PLAN")

        if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
            execute_release_plan "$release_plan" "$repo_path"
        fi
    fi

    # Cleanup
    ntm_kill_session "$session_name"

    local duration=$(($(date +%s%3N) - start_time))
    write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "agent-sweep" "success" "$duration" "" "$repo_path"
    mark_repo_completed "$repo_path"
    return 0
}
```

## Recovery Note
If something goes wrong, the backup ref can be used:
```bash
git show refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # View original HEAD
git reset --hard refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # Restore (use with care)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates backup reference before any changes
- [ ] Preflight check gates workflow entry
- [ ] All three phases execute in order
- [ ] Timeouts enforced per phase
- [ ] Plans extracted and validated
- [ ] Execution only in apply mode
- [ ] Results written for all outcomes
- [ ] Session cleaned up on completion/failure

```bash
run_single_agent_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local with_release="${2:-false}"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)

    # Preflight check
    if ! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "preflight" "skipped" 0 "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # ADDED: Create backup reference before any changes
    git -C "$repo_path" update-ref -m "agent-sweep backup before run $RUN_ID" \
        "refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-$RUN_ID" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true

    # Generate unique session name
    local session_name="ru_sweep_$(sanitize_session_name "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")")_$$"

    # Spawn session
    local spawn_result
    spawn_result=$(ntm_spawn_session "$session_name" "$repo_path")
    if ! json_get_field "$spawn_result" "success" | grep -q "true"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "spawn" "failed" 0 "session spawn failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Phase 1: Understanding
    local phase1_prompt
    phase1_prompt=$(get_phase1_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase1_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE1_TIMEOUT:-180}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase1" "timeout" "$PHASE1_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate understanding
    local pane_output
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local understanding
    understanding=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "UNDERSTANDING")

    # Phase 2: Commit Planning
    local phase2_prompt
    phase2_prompt=$(get_phase2_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase2_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE2_TIMEOUT:-300}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase2" "timeout" "$PHASE2_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate commit plan
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local commit_plan
    commit_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "COMMIT_PLAN")

    if ! validate_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "validation" "failed" 0 "commit plan validation failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Execute commit plan (if apply mode)
    if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
        if ! execute_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "execution" "failed" 0 "commit execution failed" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Phase 3: Release (optional)
    if [[ "$with_release" == true ]] && has_release_workflow "$repo_path"; then
        local phase3_prompt
        phase3_prompt=$(get_phase3_prompt "$repo_path")
        ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase3_prompt"

        if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE3_TIMEOUT:-600}"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase3" "timeout" "$PHASE3_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi

        # Extract and execute release plan
        pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
        local release_plan
        release_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "RELEASE_PLAN")

        if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
            execute_release_plan "$release_plan" "$repo_path"
        fi
    fi

    # Cleanup
    ntm_kill_session "$session_name"

    local duration=$(($(date +%s%3N) - start_time))
    write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "agent-sweep" "success" "$duration" "" "$repo_path"
    mark_repo_completed "$repo_path"
    return 0
}
```

## Recovery Note
If something goes wrong, the backup ref can be used:
```bash
git show refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # View original HEAD
git reset --hard refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # Restore (use with care)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates backup reference before any changes
- [ ] Preflight check gates workflow entry
- [ ] All three phases execute in order
- [ ] Timeouts enforced per phase
- [ ] Plans extracted and validated
- [ ] Execution only in apply mode
- [ ] Results written for all outcomes
- [ ] Session cleaned up on completion/failure

 {"id":"bd-be4n","title":"Implement worktree merge and push workflow","description":"Task: Implement Worktree Merge and Push

Purpose
-------
After quality gates pass, merge worktree changes back to main branch
and push to remote. This is the final step of the Apply phase.

Workflow
--------
1. Verify quality gates passed
2. Checkout main branch in worktree
3. Merge review branch
4. Push to remote
5. Clean up worktree

Implementation
--------------

push_worktree_changes()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local wt_path="$2"
  local plan_file="$wt_path/.ru/review-plan.json"
  
  # Get branch info
  local wt_branch base_ref
  wt_branch=$(jq -r .git.branch "$plan_file")
  base_ref=$(jq -r .git.base_ref "$plan_file")
  
  # Get main repo path
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  log_step "Merging changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Fetch worktree branch to main repo
  git -C "$main_repo" fetch "$wt_path" "$wt_branch:$wt_branch"
  
  # Checkout base and merge
  git -C "$main_repo" checkout "$base_ref"
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" merge --ff-only "$wt_branch"; then
    log_error "Cannot fast-forward merge, manual resolution needed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Push
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" push; then
    log_error "Push failed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  log_success "Pushed changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Clean up worktree branch
  git -C "$main_repo" branch -d "$wt_branch"
  
  # Record push
  record_push_completed "$repo_id" "$wt_branch"
  
  return 0

Merge Strategy
--------------
Default: --ff-only (fast-forward only)
- Safest, requires linear history
- Fails if main has new commits
- User must resolve manually

Alternative: --no-ff
- Creates merge commit
- Works with diverged history
- Configurable per-repo

Pre-Push Verification
---------------------
verify_push_safe()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Check tests passed
  local tests_ok
  tests_ok=$(jq -r .git.tests.ok "$plan_file")
  if [[ "$tests_ok" \!= "true" ]]; then
    log_error "Tests did not pass, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Check no unanswered questions
  local unanswered
  unanswered=$(jq "[.questions[] | select(.answered \!= true)] | length" "$plan_file")
  if [[ $unanswered -gt 0 ]]; then
    log_error "$unanswered unanswered questions, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  return 0

Rollback Support
----------------
If push fails or needs reverting:

rollback_worktree_merge()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  # Get the commit before merge
  local before_merge
  before_merge=$(git -C "$main_repo" rev-parse HEAD~1)
  
  # Reset to before merge
  git -C "$main_repo" reset --hard "$before_merge"
  
  log_warn "Rolled back merge for $repo_id"

Push Recording
--------------
record_push_completed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local branch="$2"
  
  update_review_state "
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push = \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\" |
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push_branch = \"$branch\"
  "

Testing
-------
- Verify ff-only merge works
- Verify merge fails gracefully on conflict
- Verify push records in state
- Test rollback procedure
- Verify worktree cleanup

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Merges worktree to main cleanly
- [ ] Pushes only with explicit --push
- [ ] Fails safely on merge conflict
- [ ] Records push in state
- [ ] Cleans up worktree branch
- [ ] Rollback procedure works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:11.392465319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:57:51.123833799-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:57:51.123833799-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be4n","depends_on_id":"bd-vcr9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.539768997-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ircy","title":"Define phase prompts with structured output markers","description":"# Phase Prompts with Structured Output

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Define the three phase prompts that produce machine-parseable JSON output.

## Phase 1 Prompt (Understanding)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT='First read AGENTS.md (if present) and README.md (if present) carefully.
If a file is missing, explicitly note that and continue.
Then use your investigation mode to understand the codebase architecture,
entrypoints, conventions, and what the current changes appear to be.
At the end, output a short structured summary as JSON between:
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_BEGIN
{ "summary": "...", "conventions": [...], "risks": [...], "notes": [...] }
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_END'
```

## Phase 2 Prompt (Commit Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT='Now, based on your knowledge of the project, DO NOT run git commands.
Instead, produce a COMMIT PLAN as JSON between these markers:
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END

Rules:
- Do not edit any code or files.
- Do not include ephemeral/ignored files (.pyc, node_modules, __pycache__, etc.).
- Group changes into logically connected commits.
- For each commit, include:
  - "files": explicit list of paths to stage
  - "message": full commit message (subject + body)
- Include "push": true/false
- Include "excluded_files": list of files excluded and why
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Phase 3 Prompt (Release Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT='If a release is warranted based on the changes, DO NOT execute release commands.
Produce a RELEASE PLAN as JSON between:
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_END

Include:
- "version": proposed version (or null if no release needed)
- "tag": proposed tag (or null)
- "changelog_entry": text to add to CHANGELOG
- "version_files": files to update with new version
- "checks": actions to verify before release (tests/CI)
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Commit Plan Schema
```json
{
  "commits": [
    {"files": ["path/a", "path/b"], "message": "feat(x): summary\n\nBody..."},
    {"files": ["path/c"], "message": "fix(y): summary\n\nBody..."}
  ],
  "push": true,
  "excluded_files": [
    {"path": "__pycache__/foo.pyc", "reason": "bytecode cache"}
  ],
  "assumptions": ["No breaking changes detected"],
  "risks": ["Large diff in core module"]
}
```

## Release Plan Schema
```json
{
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "tag": "v1.2.0",
  "changelog_entry": "## v1.2.0 (2026-01-06)\n\n### Added\n- ...",
  "version_files": [
    {"path": "VERSION", "old": "1.1.0", "new": "1.2.0"}
  ],
  "checks": ["tests", "lint"]
}
```

## Environment Variable Overrides
```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 1
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 2
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 3
```

## Per-Repo Prompt Files
Check for: $REPO_PATH/.ru/phase{1,2,3}-prompt.txt
If exists, use file contents instead of default.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:51:33.295772184-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:52:20.035151713-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:52:20.035151713-05:00","close_reason":"Phase prompts implemented by BlueRaven - AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE{1,2,3}_PROMPT constants and get_effective_phase_prompt() function with per-repo override support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ircy","depends_on_id":"bd-6nuc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T18:03:19.896537113-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-ix9c","title":"Implement run_sequential_agent_sweep()","description":"# Sequential Agent Sweep Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Process repositories one at a time (default mode, safest).

## Implementation

```bash
run_sequential_agent_sweep() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local with_release="$2"
    local success_count=0
    local fail_count=0
    
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_name repo_path session_name
        repo_name=$(basename "$repo_spec" | sed "s/@.*//")
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        session_name="ru_sweep_${repo_name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}_$$"
        
        log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
        
        # Run preflight check
        if \! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            log_warn "  Skipping: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
            write_result "$repo_name" "agent-sweep" "preflight_failed" "0" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
            continue
        fi
        
        if run_single_agent_workflow "$session_name" "$repo_path" "$with_release"; then
            log_success "  Completed: $repo_name"
            ((success_count++))
            mark_repo_completed "$repo_spec"
        else
            log_error "  Failed: $repo_name"
            ((fail_count++))
        fi
        
        save_agent_sweep_state "in_progress"
    done
    
    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=$success_count
    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=$fail_count
    
    [[ $fail_count -gt 0 ]] && return 1
    return 0
}
```

## Features
- Preflight check before each repo
- State saved after each repo (for resume)
- Clear progress logging
- Counts success/fail for summary","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:06.003069108-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:06.003069108-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-b00c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.557304053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-51fm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.585784474-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:47.606664655-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-yrod","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:55:16.040252145-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jk4n","title":"[EPIC] Security Guardrails & Validation","description":"# Security Guardrails & Validation

## Critical Insight
The prompt "Don't commit ephemeral files" is NOT sufficient. The agent can ignore it.
ru MUST enforce guardrails before executing any commit/push.

## Planner → Validator → Executor Model
1. Agent produces structured JSON plans (commits, releases)
2. ru validates plans against security rules
3. ru executes only validated plans
4. Agent NEVER directly runs git commands

## File Denylist
Before executing commit plan, validate each file against denylist:
- .env, .env.*
- *.pem, *.key, id_rsa, *.p12, *.pfx
- credentials.json, secrets.json
- node_modules, __pycache__, .pyc
- dist/, build/, *.log, .DS_Store

## File Size Limits
Default: 10MB max per file (configurable via AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB)

## Binary Detection
Detect binary files and require explicit allow:
- Uses `file -b` to check if file is text/script/json/xml

## Secret Scanning
Layered approach:
1. If gitleaks installed: Full scan
2. Elif detect-secrets installed: Scan
3. Else: Heuristic patterns (AWS keys, GitHub PATs, private keys, etc.)

Block push if secrets detected. Write artifact report. Mark repo failed.

## Commit Plan Validation
validate_commit_plan() checks:
- Files not on denylist
- Files under size limit
- No binaries without explicit allow
- Secret scan passes

## Release Plan Validation
- Version format valid
- Version files exist
- Tag format valid

## Policy
Default: Block commit on validation failure, preserve plan in artifacts for manual review.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:36.238165636-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:36.238165636-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jk4n","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.089190203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jleo","title":"Fix get_repo_status rev-list failure output","description":"Problem: get_repo_status currently emits AHEAD=? BEHIND=? when git rev-list fails (e.g., unusual repo states). cmd_status JSON output prints ahead/behind as numbers; non-numeric values can produce invalid/misleading output and may print errors.

Fix:
- Ensure get_repo_status always outputs numeric ahead/behind (use -1/-1 for unknown).
- Avoid leaking a global output var by making it local.
- Add a regression test that forces rev-list failure via a git wrapper and asserts AHEAD/BEHIND are numeric.

Acceptance:
- ru status --json prints valid JSON even when rev-list fails.
- scripts/test_local_git.sh includes a passing regression test.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T15:17:58.069291577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T15:18:08.327908375-05:00"}

@@ -16867,9 +16867,95 @@ run_sequential_agent_sweep() {
 run_parallel_agent_sweep() {
     # shellcheck disable=SC2178
     local -n repos_ref=$1
-    local max_parallel="$2"
-    # Simplified: just run sequentially for now (full impl in separate bead)
-    run_sequential_agent_sweep repos_ref
+    local max_parallel="${2:-4}"
+    local total=${#repos_ref[@]}
+
+    # For small batches, just use sequential mode
+    if [[ $total -le 1 ]] || [[ "$max_parallel" -le 1 ]]; then
+        run_sequential_agent_sweep repos_ref
+        return $?
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Starting parallel sweep: $total repos, max $max_parallel workers"
+    progress_init "$total"
+
+    # Create work queue and lock directory
+    local work_queue lock_dir
+    work_queue=$(mktemp)
+    lock_dir="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR:-/tmp}/locks_$$"
+    mkdir -p "$lock_dir"
+
+    # Populate work queue
+    printf '%s\n' "${repos_ref[@]}" > "$work_queue"
+
+    # Track worker PIDs and results
+    local -a worker_pids=()
+    local any_failed=false
+
+    # Spawn workers
+    local i
+    for ((i=0; i<max_parallel && i<total; i++)); do
+        (
+            local worker_id=$i
+            log_debug "Worker $worker_id starting"
+
+            while true; do
+                # Check global backoff before claiming work
+                agent_sweep_backoff_wait_if_needed || true
+
+                # Atomic dequeue: acquire lock, read first line, remove it
+                local repo_spec=""
+                if dir_lock_acquire "${lock_dir}/queue.lock" 30 2>/dev/null; then
+                    if [[ -s "$work_queue" ]]; then
+                        repo_spec=$(head -n1 "$work_queue" 2>/dev/null || true)
+                        if [[ -n "$repo_spec" ]]; then
+                            tail -n +2 "$work_queue" > "${work_queue}.tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
+                            mv "${work_queue}.tmp" "$work_queue" 2>/dev/null || true
+                        fi
+                    fi
+                    dir_lock_release "${lock_dir}/queue.lock" 2>/dev/null || true
+                fi
+
+                # No more work
+                [[ -z "$repo_spec" ]] && break
+
+                # Process this repo
+                local rn
+                rn=$(get_repo_name "$repo_spec")
+                progress_start_repo "$rn"
+                log_debug "Worker $worker_id processing $rn"
+
+                if run_single_agent_workflow "$repo_spec"; then
+                    record_repo_result "$rn" "success"
+                    progress_complete_repo "success"
+                else
+                    local exit_code=$?
+                    record_repo_result "$rn" "failed" "$exit_code"
+                    progress_complete_repo "failed" "exit code $exit_code"
+                fi
+            done
+
+            log_debug "Worker $worker_id finished"
+        ) &
+        worker_pids+=($!)
+    done
+
+    # Wait for all workers
+    local pid
+    for pid in "${worker_pids[@]}"; do
+        if ! wait "$pid"; then
+            any_failed=true
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Cleanup
+    rm -f "$work_queue" "${work_queue}.tmp"
+    rm -rf "$lock_dir"
+
+    # Show summary
+    progress_summary
+
+    [[ "$any_failed" != true ]]
 }
 
 run_single_agent_workflow() {
